channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Seattle police officer fired for fatally hitting graduate student with car
item.description: A Seattle police officer who struck and killed a graduate student from India with his vehicle while responding to an overdose call in 2023 has been fired.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds swept through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences Tuesday, burning homes in Pacific Palisades and prompting evacuation orders for tens of thousands.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JANIE HAR and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Second Los Angeles-area fire breaks out as firefighters battle raging Palisades inferno
item.description: California brush fires struck on Tuesday, with a second fire breaking out near Pasadena, forcing some residents to flee by foot as evacuation routes are stunted highway closures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cybertruck driver used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas attack, police say
item.description: Police found a 6-page manifesto on Matthew Livelsberger’s phone and say he used ChatGPT to plan his New Year's Day bombing at the front of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas, the LVMPD said at a news conference Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, ABC 4

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, Fox 5

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who exploded Tesla Cybertruck outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI, police say
item.description: The highly decorated soldier who exploded a Tesla Cybertruck outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas used generative AI including ChatGPT to help plan the attack, Las Vegas police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrant caravan being held at resort by Mexican authorities
item.description: Mexican government officials are breaking up migrant caravans headed for the U.S. border and sending them elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: Trump's remarks were typically wide-ranging and encompassed topics from conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine to Trump’s long-standing grievances.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans is a city marked by tragedy. But don’t call locals resilient
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — She ran around in silver sparkling shoes, her faux chainmail tunic shimmering in the freezing breeze, maneuvering horses made of paper mâché, a giant green dragon, and sheep constructed from milk cartons.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, SHARON LURYE, CLAIRE GALOFARO and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As MLK holiday nears, Bernice King urges people to really focus on his teachings
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — As the holiday honoring slain civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. approaches, his daughter is urging people to really focus on his teachings and work throughout the coming year, rather than just quoting him or celebrating for a day. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to stay safe during Southern California’s latest Santa Ana wind event
item.description: Whether you live in these communities or adjacent, officials are urging residents to take precautions to stay safe during the latest wind event.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Traffic has gotten way worse in these US cities, report finds
item.description: You're not imagining it. Traffic congestion worsened last year in just about every American city studied by navigation and GPS company TomTom.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon places new rules on homeowners living in certain high-risk wildfire areas
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Oregon homeowners living in certain areas at high risk of wildfire will face stricter building codes and mandates to reduce vegetation on their properties under new “wildfire hazard maps” unveiled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man charged with sexual abuse after incident in New York Walmart bathroom
item.description: She was eventually able to push the man back far enough to get out of the bathroom stall and report the incident.
item.dc:creator: Clare Normoyle, NewsNation

item.title: Palisades Fire in Los Angeles scorches 2,921 acres; many homes destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Tens of thousands of residents in the western Los Angeles area were under mandatory evacuation orders Tuesday evening as a wind-driven wildfire exploded in size and swept through communities in the Pacific Palisades. The Palisades Fire was first reported around 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: These will be the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York appeals court denies halting Trump's sentencing
item.description: A New York appellate judge on Tuesday declined to suspend President-elect Trump’s Friday sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KRON4

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, ABC 4

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man behind New Year's attack visited New Orleans before, recorded video with smart glasses, FBI says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The man responsible for the truck attack in New Orleans on New Year's Day that killed 14 people visited the city twice before and recorded video of the French Quarter with Meta smart glasses, an FBI official said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, STEPHEN SMITH and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, ABC 4

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for crime
item.description: A 15-year-old boy accused of armed attempted robbery was brought to Florida deputies to be turned in, by his own mother.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9
